params.text: Security.evtx record shows a successful remote logon (EventID=4624) for TargetUserName=srl-helpdesk@outlook.com with LogonType=10 and IpAddress=174.196.200.9.

params.justification: EventID 4624 with LogonType=10 is typically used for remote interactive logons (RDP/remote desktop); the record explicitly lists TargetUserName=srl-helpdesk@outlook.com and IpAddress=174.196.200.9 and ProcessName=C:\Windows\System32\svchost.exe which are consistent with network-based remote logon.
params.text: The record indicates a remote interactive/remote desktop type logon (LogonType=10) for the MicrosoftAccount srl-helpdesk@outlook.com where the process name is svchost.exe and the source IP is 174.196.200.9; this indicates an incoming RDP/remote interactive session from that IP into the host under that MicrosoftAccount identity.
params.what_would_change_this_confidence: If additional correlated logs showed this was a local SSO or service account token reuse (e.g. tokens from Azure AD Connect) rather than an external RDP session, confidence would lower; presence of Network Address 127.0.0.1 or missing IP would also reduce confidence.

cited_spans.span_text: LNK target=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst workdir=G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST

params.text: Recent LNK evidence shows SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.lnk with target path pointing to G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst, indicating a PST export was accessed.

params.justification: The LNK target explicitly contains 'G:\My Drive\...\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst', which is the typical path for Google Drive File Stream mounted paths. This implies the PST file was present under the user's Google Drive mount and was accessed via Explorer, creating the Recent LNK.
params.text: The LNK indicates the PST file was located under Google Drive path 'G:\My Drive\STARK-RESEARCH-LABS FOLDER\Exported-PST\SRL-EMAIL-EXPORT.pst', suggesting the user synced or stored the exported PST in Google Drive (My Drive) accessible to the host.
params.what_would_change_this_confidence: If the G: drive was a different network share or the LNK target was spoofed, confidence would lower; corroborating evidence from GoogleDriveFS prefetch or SRUM network usage for GoogleDrive would increase confidence.
